visa_free.notes: EU citizens have freedom of movement and may enter Croatia without a visa; entry with national ID card permitted for some. Croatia joined Schengen on 1 January 2023.

Nicaraguan ordinary passport holders remain technically visa-free for Schengen/Croatia pending a formal EU reciprocity suspension decision. Monitor official EU sources for updates.

visa_free.nationality: British nationals (Overseas) and other non-UK British nationals
visa_free.notes: Listed in Schengen Annex II under 'British citizens who are not nationals of the UK for purposes of Union law'. Covers BN(O), BOTC, BOC, BPP and British Subjects. 90 days in any 180-day period.

Listed in Schengen Annex II under entities not recognised as states by at least one member state. Exemption only applies to holders of passports issued by Taiwan which include an identity card number. 90 days in any 180-day period.

Added to Schengen Annex II by Regulation (EU) 2023/850, effective from 1 January 2024 (the earlier of ETIAS launch or 1 January 2024 - the latter occurred first). Exemption applies only to holders of biometric passports issued by Kosovo in line with ICAO standards. 90 days in any 180-day period.

UK citizens do not require a visa to enter Croatia (Schengen area). UK citizens must obtain a UK ETA before travel (this is a UK government requirement, not a Croatian visa). Passport must be valid for at least 3 months after planned departure and issued within the previous 10 years. 90 days in any 180-day period. ETIAS (EU system) is expected to launch Q4 2026 and will also be required for UK nationals.

visa_required.notes: Vanuatu was permanently moved from Schengen Annex II to Annex I (visa required) with effect from 3 February 2025 under Regulation (EU) 2025/11 of 19 December 2024, amending Regulation (EU) 2018/1806. This followed a temporary suspension (since May 2022) due to Vanuatu's investor citizenship programmes that allowed visa-required third-country nationals to obtain Vanuatu passports and bypass Schengen visa controls. This is the first time a country has been permanently removed from the Schengen visa-free list.

visa_policy.notes: Croatia is a Schengen Area member state as of 1 January 2023. Visa policy for third-country nationals is governed by EU Regulation 2018/1806 (Schengen visa regulation). Countries in Annex I require a visa; countries in Annex II are visa-exempt for stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period. Croatia does not operate a separate national visa-on-arrival or e-visa program for Schengen short stays. The EU ETIAS system is expected to launch in Q4 2026 (EUR 20 fee, raised from EUR 7; persons under 18 or over 70 exempt); when operational it will require pre-travel authorisation for Annex II nationalities (transitional period until approximately April 2027). No Croatia-specific national derogations granting additional visa-free access beyond Schengen Annex II were identified on official sources consulted. Decision No. 565/2014/EU (granting visa-free access to holders of Bulgaria/Romania/Cyprus national visas) ceased to apply to Croatia on 1 January 2023 when Croatia joined the Schengen Area - those holders now need a valid Schengen visa. Vanuatu (VUT) was permanently removed from Annex II and placed in Annex I (visa required) effective 3 February 2025 under Regulation (EU) 2025/11 of 19 December 2024. Kosovo (XKX) was added to Annex II by Regulation (EU) 2023/850, effective 1 January 2024 (biometric passports only). Nicaragua imposed visas on Croatian nationals on 16 February 2026; EU reciprocity mechanism invoked but no formal suspension of Nicaragua's Schengen visa-free access adopted as of June 2026. Georgia: EU Commission suspended visa-free travel only for holders of Georgian diplomatic, service, and official passports (effective 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027, per Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/496); Georgian ordinary passport holders remain visa-free under Annex II unchanged. Regulation (EU) 2025/2441 amended Regulation (EU) 2018/1806 to strengthen and expand the suspension mechanism (broadening grounds and reinforcing Commission's role in monitoring) but did not change any Annex I/II country listings.

credential.subtype: Type C uniform visa (two or multiple entries)

conditional_access.conditions: Uniform Schengen Type C visa must be for two or multiple entries, valid for all Schengen Area Member States. Single-entry C visas (C01) do NOT qualify. A C02 visa is not considered valid if both entries into the Schengen Area are used before entering Croatia. Visa and passport must be valid at moment of entry, during stay, and at departure. Passport valid for at least 3 months after planned departure from Croatia. Stay not to exceed 90 days in any 180-day period.

credential.subtype: Type C LTV visa (limited territorial validity, two or multiple entries)

conditional_access.conditions: Visa with limited territorial validity (LTV visa) for two or multiple entries, issued to a holder of a travel document not recognized by one or more but not all Schengen Area Member States, valid for the territory of the member states recognizing the travel document. Visa and passport must be valid at moment of entry, during stay, and at departure. Stay not to exceed 90 days in any 180-day period.

Long-stay visa (D) for stays exceeding three months, issued by one of the Schengen Area Member States. Visa and passport must be valid at moment of entry, during stay, and at departure. Passport valid for at least 3 months after planned departure from Croatia. Stay not to exceed 90 days in any 180-day period.

Residence permit issued by one of the Schengen Area Member States. Permit and passport must be valid at moment of entry, during stay, and at departure. Passport valid for at least 3 months after planned departure from Croatia. Stay not to exceed 90 days in any 180-day period.

Third-country national family members of EU/EEA/Swiss citizens holding a valid EU family member residence card ('Residence card of a family member of a Union citizen') issued under Directive 2004/38/EC may enter Croatia visa-free when travelling with or to join the EU citizen family member. Note: UK-issued residence documents for EU family members no longer qualify as UK is no longer an EU member state.

Holders of Indian diplomatic and official passports can enter, leave, transit through and stay in Croatia for up to 30 days without a visa. Based on bilateral Agreement on Exemption of Visa Requirements for Holders of Diplomatic and Official/Service Passports between the Republic of India and the Republic of Croatia, signed 19 September 2007, entered into force 8 April 2008.

Holders of Vietnamese diplomatic and official passports may enter Croatia without a visa under a bilateral Agreement on Visa Cancellation for Holders of Diplomatic and Official Passports signed in Hanoi on 29 October 2009. Exact stay duration limit not confirmed from official source; check full agreement text.

credential.subtype: travel document issued to recognised refugee or stateless person by an Annex II country

conditional_access.conditions: Recognised refugees and stateless persons who reside in an Annex II country and hold a travel document issued by that country are exempt from the visa requirement per EU Regulation 2018/1806 Article 4. Note: holders of a Certificate of Travel issued by the UK require a visa.

cbi.notes: Croatia explicitly does not grant citizenship based on investment or ownership of property/real estate in Croatia. This is confirmed on the official Ministry of Foreign Affairs citizenship page. Citizenship is acquired by origin, birth, naturalization, or international treaties. Naturalization requires residing in Croatia as well as meeting other requirements. No CBI program exists.

programs.name: Temporary Stay for Digital Nomads
programs.notes: Available to non-EU/EEA/Swiss citizens working remotely via digital communication for a foreign company or their own foreign-registered company. Cannot work for Croatian employers. Duration: up to 18 months (extendable by 6 months). Income requirement: minimum EUR 3,622.50/month (2.5x average Croatian monthly net salary), or lump sum EUR 43,470 for 12 months / EUR 65,205 for 18 months. Reapplication earliest 6 months after previous stay expires. Does not count toward permanent residence under standard rules.

programs.name: Self-Employment Residence and Work Permit (min 51% company ownership)
programs.notes: Third-country nationals may obtain residence and work permit for self-employment in a company in which they have an ownership share of at least 51%, or in a trade in which they have an ownership share of at least 51%. No minimum investment threshold specified in the official source. Standard temporary stay (up to 1 year, renewable). After 5 uninterrupted years of lawful residence, may qualify for long-term residence. Minimum investment amount was not stated on the official page.

programs.name: EU Blue Card (Highly Qualified Workers)
programs.notes: Permit for highly qualified workers with at least a higher education diploma, working under employment contract for minimum 1 year in Croatia. Salary must be at least 1.5x the average gross annual salary in Croatia. Validity: aligned to employment contract (minimum 1 year), now up to 48 months per 2025 amendments. Path to long-term residence after 5 years (may aggregate time in multiple EU states with minimum 2 years in Croatia). Processing time: 60-day statutory deadline.

programs.name: Long-Term Residence (general)
programs.notes: Available after 5 years of uninterrupted lawful temporary stay in Croatia. Requirements: valid travel document, means of self-support, health insurance, knowledge of Croatian language and Latin script. Absences up to 10 months total over 5 years (single absence max 6 months) do not break continuity. After long-term residence, path to citizenship via naturalization may be pursued.

programs.name: EU Blue Card - Highly Qualified Worker
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals with higher education diploma (or equivalent professional experience for ICT roles), employment contract in Croatia for at least 1 year, salary at least 1.5x average gross annual salary in Croatia
programs.notes: No labor market test required for EU Blue Card. Valid for up to 48 months (as of 2025 amendments, up from previous 24 months). Highly qualified ICT professionals may use work experience instead of formal education qualification. Accelerated biometric card processing available for additional fee.

programs.name: Temporary Stay for Digital Nomads
programs.eligibility: Non-EU/EEA/Swiss nationals working remotely via digital technology for a foreign employer or own foreign-registered company. Cannot provide services to Croatian clients. Minimum monthly income EUR 3,622.50.
programs.notes: Application via Croatian diplomatic missions (for visa-required nationals) or online/police station (for visa-exempt nationals). Fee at embassy: EUR 55.74 (temporary stay) + EUR 93.00 (visa D) or EUR 41.14 (biometric card). Fee at police station: EUR 46.45 + EUR 41.14.

outbound_official.notes: Source is the Croatian Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs (MVEP) interactive Visa Requirements Overview tool, which provides per-country visa status for Croatian ordinary passport holders. All 195 destination countries listed in the dropdown were checked via the ?country=N URL parameter. Categories reflect what the MVEP page states for 'Citizen of the Republic of Croatia / Ordinary passport'. Where a page states both visa on arrival and e-visa options the entry is classified visa_on_arrival (practical easiest access). Belarus listed as visa_free based on the July 2024 visa-free regime introduced until December 31 2025 - verify whether this has been extended. Iran listed as visa_free (up to 15 days no visa) with VOA option for 30 days. Mongolia listed as visa_free (ordinary passport holders may enter without visa for up to 30 days for tourism). Korea Republic listed as visa_free because K-ETA requirement is currently suspended until December 31 2026. Kuwait has an active suspension of visa issuance; status listed as visa_on_arrival per the page text but this may not be operative in practice. Nicaragua imposed mandatory entry visas on Croatian nationals as of February 16 2026 and now requires e-visa.

visa_free.name: Belarus
visa_free.note: Visa-free since July 19 2024 for max 30 days; regime introduced until December 31 2025 - verify current status

visa_free.name: Cape Verde
visa_free.note: No visa for tourism max 30 days; mandatory online registration at ease.gov.cv before travel

visa_free.name: Iran
visa_free.note: No visa for tourist stays up to 15 days within 180 days; visa on arrival also available for stays up to 30 days at major airports

visa_free.name: Korea, Republic of
visa_free.note: K-ETA normally required but exemption valid until December 31 2026 by decision of Korean Government

visa_free.name: Mongolia
visa_free.note: Ordinary passport holders may enter without visa for up to 30 days for tourism

visa_free.name: Sao Tome and Principe
visa_free.note: No visa for stays up to 15 days; e-visa required for longer stays

visa_free.name: Seychelles
visa_free.note: No visa required but mandatory travel form at seychelles.govtas.com before travel

visa_on_arrival.name: Bahrain
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival max 14 days (fee 25 BHD); e-visa also available at e-visa.gov.bh

visa_on_arrival.name: Bangladesh
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival max 30 days

visa_on_arrival.name: Burundi
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival max 30 days

visa_on_arrival.name: Comoros
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival max 45 days

visa_on_arrival.name: Djibouti
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa at the border

visa_on_arrival.name: Egypt
visa_on_arrival.note: E-visa via visa2egypt.gov.eg (primary); visa on arrival only for organized tourist charter trips to tourist destinations (not Cairo/Alexandria)

visa_on_arrival.name: Guyana
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival for tourist stays up to 30 days

visa_on_arrival.name: Jamaica
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa obtainable at Jamaican Diplomatic Mission/Consular Office or at border crossing; US permanent residents may visit without visa

visa_on_arrival.name: Jordan
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival available; also e-visa at eservices.moi.gov.jo

visa_on_arrival.name: Kuwait
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival for max 3 months; NOTE: suspension of visa issuance currently in force - verify current status before travel

visa_on_arrival.name: Lebanon
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival at airport or naval port for up to 30 days (free); extendable to 90 days

visa_on_arrival.name: Madagascar
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival max 90 days

visa_on_arrival.name: Maldives
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival max 30 days

visa_on_arrival.name: Mozambique
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival at major airports max 30 days; e-visa also available at evisa.gov.mz

visa_on_arrival.name: Namibia
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival at Hosea Kutako and Walvis Bay airports and listed land borders max 90 days; e-portal also available at eservices.mhaiss.gov.na

visa_on_arrival.name: Oman
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa obtainable at Omani border crossing or diplomatic missions

visa_on_arrival.name: Qatar
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival for tourist visit, free of charge

visa_on_arrival.name: Saudi Arabia
visa_on_arrival.note: E-visa at visa.visitsaudi.com (primary); visa on arrival also available for tourists for max 90 days

visa_on_arrival.name: Somalia
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival for tourist stays up to 30 days

visa_on_arrival.name: Sri Lanka
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival max 30 days; ETA also required before arrival at eta.gov.lk

visa_on_arrival.name: Tanzania
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival or e-visa at eservices.immigration.go.tz

visa_on_arrival.name: Uganda
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa on arrival; e-visa also available at visas.immigration.go.ug

visa_on_arrival.name: Zambia
visa_on_arrival.note: Visa at the border for tourist visits up to 90 days or business up to 30 days

e_visa.name: Australia
e_visa.note: eVisitor subclass 651, free, apply at homeaffairs.gov.au

e_visa.name: Azerbaijan
e_visa.note: ASAN e-visa system, $20 USD, single entry up to 30 days

e_visa.name: Nicaragua
e_visa.note: Nicaragua imposed mandatory entry visas on Croatian nationals as of February 16 2026; e-visa at tramites.migob.gob.ni

e_visa.name: Suriname
e_visa.note: E-visa or e-tourist card at suriname.vfsevisa.com

eta.name: Israel
eta.note: ETA-IL mandatory from January 1 2025; apply at israel-entry.piba.gov.il; ordinary passport holders do not need a prior consular visa

eta.name: Kenya
eta.note: Electronic Travel Authorization mandatory; apply at etakenya.go.ke at least 3 days before travel; fee $30 USD; visa-free regime announced January 2024

eta.name: New Zealand
eta.note: NZeTA mandatory from October 1 2019; apply at immigration.govt.nz; NZD $9 (app) or $12 (web); ordinary passport holders do not need a prior consular visa

eta.name: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland
eta.note: ETA mandatory from April 2 2025; apply at gov.uk/electronic-travel-authorisation; ordinary passport holders do not need a prior consular visa

validation_notes: CORRECTED 2026-06-27: Kosovo (XKX) ADDED to visa_policy.visa_free. Kosovo was transferred from Annex I to Annex II of Regulation (EU) 2018/1806 by Regulation (EU) 2023/850, effective 1 January 2024. Biometric passports only; 90 days in any 180-day period. The prior record was missing this entry entirely - Kosovo appeared only in outbound_official.visa_free but not in the inbound visa_free list. Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/850/oj/eng

VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Nicaragua (NIC) remains in visa_free with warning note. As of June 2026, no formal EU Council decision suspending Nicaragua's Schengen visa-free access has been adopted following the reciprocity mechanism invocation. Record kept as-is with existing warning note.

VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Consolidated Reg 2018/1806 as of 3 February 2025 confirms Vanuatu in Annex I, Kosovo in Annex II, Serbia biometric requirement clarification. No other Annex changes found. Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:02018R1806-20250203

VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Georgia ordinary passport visa-free entry unchanged. The European Commission suspended visa-free travel only for holders of Georgian diplomatic, service, and official passports (effective March 6, 2026 to March 6, 2027). Ordinary passport holders remain visa-free under Annex II.

VERIFIED 2026-06-27: ETIAS confirmed Q4 2026 launch (EUR 20 fee). Transitional period of approximately 6 months after launch before mandatory enforcement. Notes updated to include transitional period detail.

CORRECTED: Vanuatu (VUT) removed from visa_free list. Vanuatu was permanently moved from Schengen Annex II to Annex I (visa required) effective 3 February 2025 under Regulation (EU) 2025/11. The prior record had Vanuatu listed as visa-free with only a caveat note. Vanuatu now requires a visa to enter Croatia/Schengen.

CORRECTED: Nicaragua (NIC) note updated to reflect that Nicaragua unilaterally imposed visa requirements on Croatian nationals on 16 February 2026. The EU reciprocity mechanism under Regulation (EU) 2018/1806 has been invoked (European Parliament question P-10-2026-000868 filed March 2026), but as of June 2026 no EU Council decision formally suspending Nicaragua's Schengen visa-free access has been confirmed. Nicaragua remains in visa_free with a prominent warning note.

CORRECTED: All Annex II source_url references updated from the outdated PDF path (home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2020-05/regulation-2018-1806.pdf) to the live EUR-Lex HTML version of Regulation (EU) 2018/1806.

CORRECTED: The duplicate conditional_access entries (indices 7 and 8 in the prior record) for EU family member residence cards and Schengen D-visa/residence permit were merged/deduplicated into single authoritative entries.

CORRECTED: The eta[] array has been cleared. The prior record incorrectly placed a UK ETA entry in Croatia's eta section. The UK ETA is a UK government document requirement for UK citizens travelling abroad - it is not a Croatian entry requirement and should not appear in Croatia's visa_policy.eta field.
CORRECTED: UK entry in visa_free updated to remove the misleading ETA framing and note that ETIAS (EU system, Q4 2026 launch, EUR 20 fee) will also apply to UK nationals when operational.
VERIFIED: Croatia has no CBI programme - confirmed unchanged.

VERIFIED: Decision 565/2014/EU (Bulgaria/Cyprus/Romania national visas) ceased to apply to Croatia as of 1 January 2023 upon Schengen accession. The MVEP page (125335) still references this but it is legally no longer applicable to Croatia.

VERIFIED: ETIAS confirmed launching Q4 2026 per European Commission revised timeline (March 2025 announcement). Fee revised to EUR 20 (raised from EUR 7; persons under 18 or over 70 exempt). Not yet operational as of June 2026.
VERIFIED: All RBI and fast_track program details unchanged - no new official information found contradicting prior record.

CORRECTED 2026-06-29: visa_required.source_url corrected from https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=OJ:L_202402059 (the 2024 temporary suspension delegated regulation) to https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2025/11/oj/eng (Regulation (EU) 2025/11 of 19 December 2024 — the actual permanent Annex II-to-Annex-I transfer regulation for Vanuatu, effective 3 February 2025).

VERIFIED 2026-06-29: Georgia ordinary passport holders confirmed visa-free for Schengen/Croatia. Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/496 suspended visa-free travel only for Georgian holders of diplomatic, service, and official passports (6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027). Official source: https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_26_564. Inbound visa_free Georgia entry unchanged.

VERIFIED 2026-06-29: Nicaragua reciprocity mechanism — no formal EU Council or Commission decision suspending Nicaraguan Schengen visa-free access confirmed as of 29 June 2026. Nicaragua ordinary passport holders remain technically visa-free for Schengen. Warning note in record is accurate.

VERIFIED 2026-06-29: ETIAS launch confirmed Q4 2026. Fee is EUR 20 (not EUR 7 as originally legislated — fee raised per Commission announcement). Source: https://travel-europe.europa.eu/en/etias. Notes updated to clarify EUR 20 fee and under-18/over-70 exemption.

VERIFIED 2026-06-29: Regulation (EU) 2025/2441 amended the visa suspension mechanism (broader grounds, stronger Commission role) but did NOT change Annex I or Annex II country lists. No new country additions or removals to Schengen visa-free list after February 2025 confirmed. Source: eur-lex.europa.eu search.

VERIFIED 2026-06-29: No new country added to Schengen Annex II after Kosovo (Reg 2023/850, effective Jan 2024) and before this validation run. No Dominican Republic, no new Pacific island additions, no other changes found.
gaps: Croatia's Ministry of Foreign Affairs visa requirements tool (mvep.gov.hr) uses a dropdown-based interactive interface; the full per-country visa status matrix could not be extracted in bulk - only spot-checks performed.

Croatia-specific national derogations from EU Regulation 2018/1806 (if any countries beyond Schengen Annex II that Croatia independently exempts) could not be confirmed from official sources; the EU Commission's national derogations PDF (January 2024) was not accessible.
Minimum investment amount for self-employment residence permit (51% ownership route) not stated on official Invest Croatia or MUP pages.
Naturalization processing time and exact residency requirement duration for standard naturalization not explicitly stated on official MFA citizenship page.
Stay limit for Vietnamese diplomatic/official passport holders not specified in the press release source; full agreement text not found on official Croatian government domain.

Full list of countries with which Croatia has bilateral visa cancellation agreements for diplomatic/service/official passport holders not available on official MVEP pages; only India and Vietnam confirmed.
Exact EU Council decision date (if any) formally triggering reciprocity suspension for Nicaragua not yet confirmed as of June 2026.
Salary threshold for EU Blue Card in absolute EUR terms (1.5x average gross annual salary) not given as specific figure on official MUP page.
ETIAS exact go-live date within Q4 2026 not yet confirmed - European Commission states final date will be communicated closer to launch.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder exemption for Croatia found on any official source; OCI cardholders appear to require a standard Schengen visa.

visa_types.name: Airport Transit Visa (Type A)
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to transit through the international transit area of a Croatian airport without entering Croatian territory.
visa_types.notes: Schengen Type A visa issued by Croatian diplomatic missions and consular offices since Croatia joined Schengen on 1 January 2023. Required only for nationals of certain countries that do not qualify for visa-free transit.

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Schengen Visa (Type C)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to enter and stay in Croatia (and the broader Schengen Area) for purposes including tourism, business visits, family visits, or cultural/sporting events for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
visa_types.notes: Schengen uniform Type C visa valid across all Schengen member states. Issued by Croatian diplomatic missions for nationals of Schengen Annex I countries. May be single or multiple entry. Visa fee set by EU Schengen Visa Code (currently EUR 90 for adults, EUR 45 for children 6-12). Short-stay extensions possible at police stations for force majeure/serious personal reasons (EUR 30 fee). Cannot exceed 90 days in any 180-day period.

visa_types.name: National Long-Stay Visa (Type D)
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals who have been granted a temporary stay or work permit in Croatia to enter the country for stays exceeding 90 days, bridging until the residence permit is issued.
visa_types.notes: Croatian national Type D visa issued by Croatian diplomatic missions and consular offices since 1 January 2023. Valid only for Croatia; does not grant access to other Schengen states for more than 90 days. Typically issued to those who have already been granted a residence or work permit in Croatia. Max stay is up to 30 days on the visa itself; the underlying permit governs long-term stay.

visa_types.name: Stay and Work Permit (Single Permit)
visa_types.purpose: A unified authorisation allowing third-country nationals to temporarily reside and work in Croatia under a specific employer and occupation.
visa_types.notes: Single permit combining residence and work authorisation. Application submitted to competent police administration at employer's location. Fee: EUR 74.32 for permit + EUR 31.85 biometric card (EUR 59.73 expedited) + EUR 9.29 administrative fee. Labour market test required unless position is on deficit occupations list. Permit holder may work only for the specified employer and occupation. Employer fines EUR 6,630-19,900 per violation.

visa_types.name: Work Registration Certificate (Short-Term Work)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term authorisation for third-country nationals to work in Croatia for up to 90 days or 30 days per calendar year without a full residence permit.
visa_types.notes: Available in two durations: up to 90 days or up to 30 days annually. Fee: EUR 39.82. Applicants must hold valid travel document and employment contract. Does not confer residence rights beyond existing visa/visa-free entitlement.

visa_types.name: Seasonal Worker Permit
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals to work in seasonal industries (agriculture, forestry, hospitality, tourism) in Croatia for limited periods each calendar year.
visa_types.notes: Two sub-types: up to 90 days annually (no labour market test required) and up to 9 months (requires labour market test unless deficit occupation). Applications submitted via Croatian Employment Service (HZZ) platform. Covers agriculture, forestry, hospitality, and tourism sectors.

visa_types.name: EU Blue Card (Highly Qualified Worker Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Residence and work permit for highly qualified third-country nationals with higher education qualifications working under a minimum one-year employment contract in Croatia.
visa_types.notes: Requires higher education diploma or equivalent, employment contract for minimum 1 year, salary at least 1.5x average gross annual salary in Croatia. Valid for the duration of the employment contract; as of 2025 amendments, can be up to 48 months. No labour market test required. Fee: EUR 74.32 permit + EUR 31.85 biometric card (EUR 59.73 expedited) + EUR 9.29 administrative. Path to long-term residence after 5 years (may aggregate time across EU states with minimum 2 years in Croatia). Statutory processing deadline: 60 days.

visa_types.name: Temporary Stay for Digital Nomads
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals who work remotely via digital technology for a foreign employer or their own foreign-registered company to reside in Croatia for an extended period.
visa_types.notes: Available to non-EU/EEA/Swiss citizens only. Cannot work for Croatian employers or clients. Initial stay up to 18 months; extendable by 6 months if applied 60 days before expiry. New application possible 6 months after previous stay ends. Income requirement: minimum EUR 3,622.50/month (2.5x average Croatian monthly net salary), or lump sum EUR 43,470 for 12 months / EUR 65,205 for 18 months. Required documents: valid passport, health insurance, employment/business proof, bank statements, criminal record clearance, Croatian address. Fees: EUR 46.45 (temporary stay) + EUR 31.85 biometric card (EUR 59.73 expedited) + EUR 9.29 administrative. Visa-required nationals apply via embassy (additional Type D visa EUR 93). Online application available for visa-exempt nationals. Does not count toward permanent residence.

visa_types.name: Temporary Stay for Education (Student Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to reside in Croatia for the purpose of secondary school education or university studies.
visa_types.notes: Granted for up to 1 year, renewable. Applicants must provide proof of enrolment, valid travel document with 3+ months validity beyond permit expiry, proof of financial self-sufficiency, health insurance, and clean criminal record. Fees: EUR 46.45 (temporary stay approval) + EUR 31.85 biometric card (EUR 59.73 expedited) + EUR 9.29 administrative.

visa_types.name: Temporary Stay for Family Reunification
visa_types.purpose: Allows close family members (spouses, common-law partners, minor children, parents of minor Croatian citizens) of Croatian nationals or lawfully residing third-country nationals to join them in Croatia.
visa_types.notes: Eligible family members: spouses and common-law partners, minor children (biological, adopted, step), parents of minor Croatian citizens. Family members of seasonal workers generally ineligible. Documents required: marriage/birth/partnership certificates; common-law partnership can be proved by shared child's birth certificate, 3 years cohabitation proof, or witness statements. Family members of Croatian nationals exempt from providing certain documents (obtained ex officio). Fees: EUR 46.45 + EUR 31.85 biometric card + EUR 9.29 administrative.

visa_types.name: Temporary Stay for Scientific Research
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country national researchers with a hosting agreement with a Croatian research institution to reside in Croatia to conduct scientific research.
visa_types.notes: Requires hosting agreement with a Croatian research institution. Fees: EUR 46.45 + EUR 31.85 biometric card + EUR 9.29 administrative. Renewable annually.

visa_types.name: Temporary Stay for Humanitarian Grounds
visa_types.purpose: Grants residence to third-country nationals who cannot return to their home country due to health crises, age-related issues, or as victims of domestic violence.
visa_types.notes: Covers health crises, age-related inability to leave, and domestic violence victims. Applied for at police administration. Fees: EUR 46.45 + EUR 31.85 biometric card + EUR 9.29 administrative.

visa_types.name: Self-Employment Residence and Work Permit (Business Owner)
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals who own at least 51% of a Croatian-registered company or trade to reside and conduct business in Croatia.
visa_types.notes: Requires minimum 51% ownership stake in a Croatian company or trade. No minimum investment threshold specified in official sources. Exempt from labour market test under Article 110 of the Aliens Act. Renewable annually. After 5 years of uninterrupted lawful residence, may qualify for long-term residence. Fee: EUR 74.32 permit + EUR 31.85 biometric card + EUR 9.29 administrative.

visa_types.name: Long-Term Residence Permit (EU)
visa_types.purpose: Grants stable, long-term EU residence status to third-country nationals who have legally resided in Croatia for at least 5 uninterrupted years.
visa_types.notes: Requires 5 years uninterrupted lawful temporary stay. Absences up to 10 months total over 5 years (single absence max 6 months) permitted. Also requires: valid travel document, financial self-sufficiency, health insurance, knowledge of Croatian language and Latin script. Language exemptions for children under school age, those educated in Croatia, and persons over 65. Fee: EUR 83.62 decision + EUR 31.85 biometric card (EUR 59.73 expedited) + EUR 13.94 certified copy.

visa_types.name: Permanent Stay Permit
visa_types.purpose: Grants permanent residence rights in Croatia to qualifying third-country nationals including family members of Croatian citizens, diaspora members, long-term refugees, and others.
visa_types.notes: Eligible categories: (1) family members/life partners of Croatian citizens after 4 years uninterrupted temporary stay; (2) Croatian diaspora members or stateless persons after 3 years; (3) refugees with 10+ years status; (4) minor children of permanent/long-term residents after 3 years; (5) returnees in housing programs; (6) children born in Croatia without regulated status. Requires valid travel document, no security or public health threat. Fee: EUR 83.62 + EUR 31.85 biometric card.
